item.text: I’ll build a leakage-safe annotation ensemble: first characterize the test set, then retrieve established precomputed effect scores (prioritizing CADD and missense-specific predictors), add consequence/conservation signals, calibrate without using any exact test tuple as labeled training data, and validate all output constraints. I’ll record every external dataset/API and each attempted iteration in the manifest.

item.text: The first API probe confirms the service’s primary variant identifiers are hg19-based, while the supplied coordinates are hg38. I’m therefore lifting coordinates only for annotation lookup (preserving the required hg38 output keys), and I’ll reject ambiguous or allele-inconsistent mappings rather than silently attaching the wrong score.

from pyliftover import LiftOver
import pandas as pd
p=pd.read_parquet('test.parquet')
lo=LiftOver('hg38ToHg19.over.chain.gz')
out=[]
for r in p.itertuples():
 x=lo.convert_coordinate('chr'+r.chrom,r.pos-1)
 out.append(x[0] if len(x)==1 else None)
print('unique mapped',sum(x is not None for x in out),'minus',sum(x and x[2]=='-' for x in out))
print(out[:10])
PY"

item.text: The public TraitGym coordinate tables overlap this benchmark and can identify the two GWAS-style partitions without using their labels. I’m using only source/consequence metadata from those exact rows; any model fitting or validation will exclude every exact test tuple. This lets the ensemble treat regulatory and coding variants appropriately without contaminating the target labels.

item.text: CADD v1.7’s remote whole-genome file is queryable but unstable under thousands of sparse HTTP range requests, so I’m retaining the successful v1.7 probe as an iteration and switching the production pass to the batched MyVariant annotation service. That provides near-complete CADD coverage plus AlphaMissense, REVEL, ClinPred, MetaRNN, BayesDel, and population frequency in tens of batch calls.
